_meta.description: R2A sonic fingerprint test: Wu-Tang Clan (East Coast hip-hop, difficulty: Very Hard). No artist name in caption. 3 seeds. Locked 2026-05-17.

captions.ace_step: 1990s East Coast boom-bap hip-hop, 92 BPM, dusty soul sample loop with vinyl crackle, sparse upright piano stab on the two and four, raw kick-snare drum break sample with prominent hi-hat, deep upright bass under the loop, multiple male rapper voices trading verses with distinct intricate flows, occasional kung fu film sample interlude, gritty New York street narrative mood, lo-fi sample-based production with mono compression

lyrics:
[Intro]

[Verse]
Concrete teaching mathematics that the schoolbooks never had
Brother on the corner cooking up a different kind of math
Sirens in the distance like an alarm clock for the kingdom
Every rooftop holding council, every alley holding court

[Chorus]
We the ones the city built around
We the ones the city tried to drown
We the ones still standing in the sound
And we ain't going nowhere right now

[Verse]
Pops told me real recognize real before I knew his real name
Mama held the family on a wage that wouldn't fit the frame
Every cousin got a story that the news ain't never telling
Every block remembers names that nobody else is spelling

[Chorus]
We the ones the city built around
We the ones the city tried to drown
We the ones still standing in the sound
And we ain't going nowhere right now
